methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DroneShield is a public-stage company in the Defense & Security sector that has raised over $100M. The valuation universe includes 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, anchored by companies including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI across the broader frontier-defense technology landscape.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable to this analysis. Despite identifying 21 same-sector peers within Defense & Security, filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for statistical reliability. A cross-sector median would introduce material distortion given the specialized nature of defense-technology valuation and the heterogeneity of peer risk profiles, margins, and growth trajectories.

The absence of a tight peer set reflects structural constraints in the Defense & Security frontier-tech market: few companies operate at DroneShield's stage and scale with comparable business models and revenue maturity. Without sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting valuation-tier criteria, traditional median-multiple methods cannot be responsibly applied. Valuation work on this name would require alternative approaches—discounted cash flow analysis anchored to contract pipeline visibility, precedent transaction analysis in counter-drone systems, or transaction multiples from defense M&A—paired with explicit sensitivity to regulatory approval risk, international export constraints, and customer concentration. These gaps should drive further diligence before assigning a point estimate.
